title: Canonical reconstruction: semantic segments and casualty profiles
description: The single canonical reconstruction of a tactical slice (plan sections 6.3 and 6.4). Semantic segments replace unqualified straight-line interpolation; casualty profiles carry aggregate loss without inventing individual fates. Times are seconds on the canonical battle clock; geometry is battlefield-local meters (x east, z north, Unity world XZ). Cross-field rules (segment contiguity per unit, claim/obstacle/unit reference integrity, route/speed/obstacle discipline, strength reconciliation) are enforced by scripts/validate_reconstruction.py. Deviation from the plan's illustrative shape, recorded: segments carry obstacleIds (array) instead of a single obstacleId because the Emmitsburg Road is flanked by TWO traced fences that one historical crossing action traverses.

basis.description: How the slice-start strength was derived (e.g. macro track linear interpolation at t0, rounded).

colorParty.description: angle-v2 P4: length of the unit's color-guard succession chain (ColorGuard.cs). The chain is front-rank files walking outward from the line's center; the compiled casualty schedule fells bearers and the next chain man takes the colors up. Cited at the grain the evidence gives (Fry: brigade-grain sum of the attested per-regiment bearer-down counts — a disclosed simplification). No bearer is ever a named person (V&R §1).

mountedOfficers.description: angle-v2 P5 (ED-81, ADOPTED): mounted-officer figures staged with the unit (MountedOfficer.cs). The figure is anonymous by construction; attaching one to a named officer's documented moment requires the citations here (V&R §1: the render shows A mounted officer fall where the record puts one; it never labels the figure).

officerId.description: Stable hash identity — a unit-scoped id, NEVER a person's name.

backOffsetM.description: Meters behind the line in the unit formation frame (negative = a forward station in front of the line).

alongOffsetM.description: Meters along the frontage from the line's center (+right).

meleeOpponentId.description: melee segments only (angle-v2 P3): the enemy unit this melee fights. Wired SYMMETRICALLY (both sides name each other) for grapple pairs to form; a melee without it (or with one-sided wiring) resolves to unpaired bout work. Validated in code: must be a reconstruction unit on the other side whose own melee window overlaps.

route.description: Unit-centroid polyline in battlefield-local meters. A single point means the unit holds that position for the whole segment.

obstacleIds.description: Landcover feature ids (stone_wall / rail_fence lines) this segment is authored to cross. Required whenever the route intersects a traced obstacle; validated geometrically.

facingDeg.description: Optional authored facing (compass degrees, 0 = north/+Z, 90 = east/+X). When absent the compiler derives facing from route bearing; static segments inherit the previous facing.

facingToDeg.description: Optional facing at segment end; the compiler interpolates shortest-arc from facingDeg (or inherited facing) across the segment. Used for authored wheels.

inferenceRules.description: Named connective-reconstruction rules this segment relies on. A segment with no claimIds MUST name at least one rule (editorial connective reconstruction); enforced in code.

causeMix.description: Fractions by broad cause class; must sum to 1.0 within 1e-6 (code rule). Never per-person, never named individuals.

strikeCorrelation.description: angle-v2 P2 (proposed ED-82): this profile's victims of the named cause classes are re-timed by the compiler to cluster at the resolver's compiled StrikeEvents (canister/shell arrivals) instead of falling on the smooth inverse-CDF curve. The compiler emits the resulting per-victim fallTimes into the bundle; counts, victim identity, causes, and the profile window are UNCHANGED (the redistribution stays within [t0, t1] and within windowS seconds of each victim's smooth draw, so the cited intensity shape is preserved in the aggregate).
